primary_question: Did procedural commitment generalize beyond known shells while reducing scalar/direct substitution?

required_primary_telemetry.name: read_file_exact_valid_emergence
required_primary_telemetry.definition: Exact-valid rate on read_file expected-tool rows

required_primary_telemetry.name: paraphrastic_exact_valid_rate
required_primary_telemetry.definition: Exact-valid rate on paraphrastic anchor bucket

required_primary_telemetry.name: anti_scalar_generalization_delta
required_primary_telemetry.definition: Change in scalar/direct substitution shares vs i9 baseline

required_primary_telemetry.name: unseen_shell_exact_valid_rate
required_primary_telemetry.definition: Exact-valid rate for eval prompt skeletons absent from i10 intervention shells

required_secondary_telemetry.name: canonical_anchor_independence
required_secondary_telemetry.definition: Literal-anchor exact share among exact-valid outputs
required_secondary_telemetry.guardrail: must not rise materially and should remain below dependence boundary

required_secondary_telemetry.name: cross_family_transfer_index
required_secondary_telemetry.definition: Performance spread across read_file/rg_search archetypes and anchor buckets
required_secondary_telemetry.guardrail: avoid gains isolated to one archetype or one shell family

required_secondary_telemetry.name: procedural_diversity_success_ratio
required_secondary_telemetry.guardrail: should not collapse while lexical diversity rises

false_progress_disambiguation.diagnosis_rule: Lexical-up + procedural-flat/down => ritualization risk, not generalization
